violations.description: ATDD API scenarios reuse shared story context and persistent number mappings without explicit teardown in-file.
violations.suggestion: Add explicit cleanup hooks or isolated seeded identifiers per test to reduce cross-run residue.
violations.code_snippet: const mappingResponse = await apiRequest(request, { method: 'POST', path: storyE1Context.paths.numbersCollection, ... });

violations.description: Ambiguity-routing scenario seeds two tenant mappings and relies on persisted state, which increases cleanup sensitivity.
violations.suggestion: Add teardown for seeded tenant mappings or reset fixtures per case.
violations.code_snippet: const firstMapping = await apiRequest(request, { ... }); const secondMapping = await apiRequest(request, { ... });

recommendations: Introduce cleanup utilities for mapping records created in tests
Prefer unique per-test seeded entities in ATDD API file similar to automate deterministic helpers
Document fixture teardown guarantees for story-level context fixtures
summary: Isolation is strong overall, with moderate risk from persistent seeded mapping data across runs.
